danger_signals.visual_form: varied creature and robot designs scattered throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: direct combat threat requiring player action

danger_signals.visual_form: large, distinct enemy at level end with unique visual design
danger_signals.meaning: critical checkpoint; defeating boss advances progression

danger_signals.visual_form: environmental hazards integrated into level design
danger_signals.meaning: spatial danger requiring platforming precision to avoid damage

danger_signals.visual_form: visual depletion of health meter for Alisia or active pet
danger_signals.meaning: active damage state; continued hits lead to entity removal or game over

danger_signals.visual_form: Baldour's cocoon as central environmental/narrative element
danger_signals.meaning: world-state threat; cocoon awakening represents apocalyptic danger

safety_signals.visual_form: hidden items placed throughout first seven stages
safety_signals.meaning: resource recovery; enables healing, stat increases, invulnerability

safety_signals.visual_form: active pet flying alongside Alisia with visible attacks
safety_signals.meaning: damage mitigation; pets block enemy attacks and deal damage

safety_signals.visual_form: full power meter enabling multi-target attack
safety_signals.meaning: offensive readiness; capable of clearing screen of threats
pet restoration; restores removed pet to active play

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: four distinct pet monsters (Dragon Frye, Boomerang Lizard, Thunder Raven, Ball O' Fire) as allies

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: dormant cocoon as central symbol of evil presence
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: mixed creature types (zombies, dragons, robots) reflecting science fiction/fantasy blend
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: high-tech spaceships and robots coexist with mythical creatures; suggests Baldour's corrupting influence spans both technological and magical domains

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: agent of cocoon transport; operates as antagonist force
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: transported cocoon to Earth; represents immediate tactical threat to world

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: eight side-scrolling levels with mixed enemies; environments reflect both sci-fi and fantasy corruption
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Alisia progresses through escalating enemy types; world remains partially stable but visibly degraded by prior Baldour devastation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: narrative framing communicated through manual text; no direct visual manifestation in gameplay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: motivates Alisia's quest; establishes emotional stakes for player

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: cinematic cutscene of Alisia's triumphant return home
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: world narrative resolves; cocoon destroyed, evil threat eliminated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: environments and enemy presence suggest world has not fully recovered from Baldour's previous devastation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: urgency reinforced; Ornah's cocoon transport indicates renewed threat during vulnerable period

environmental_storytelling.example: simultaneous presence of high-tech spaceships/robots and mythical zombies/dragons communicates Baldour's corrupting influence spans technology and magic; inspired by Miyazaki's Nausicaä aesthetic
collectibles scattered throughout first seven stages (mostly hidden) reward exploration and suggest resource scarcity in corrupted world
each level-end boss has unique visual design reinforcing progression through distinct corrupted territories
four distinct monster companions (fire, boomerang, thunder, burning) each with unique visual attack patterns; suggests natural/magical ecosystem enlisted against evil

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: shoots lightning streaks from hands; attack auto-targets enemies in range; power depletes with each volley
unleashes multi-target screen-clearing attack when fully charged
life bar depletes; continues available allow level restart
fly autonomously around Alisia, attack enemies independently, block incoming damage
removed from play; cannot return until Revive power-up collected
spawn throughout levels, require lightning attacks or pet damage to defeat
positioned at level-end as mandatory combat checkpoint; defeat triggers level progression

notes: Alisia Dragoon communicates world-state primarily through: (1) mixed visual aesthetic blending sci-fi and fantasy to signal pervasive corruption, (2) enemy variety and placement to indicate inhabited threat landscape, (3) power-up scarcity to reinforce resource vulnerability, (4) pet mechanic as tactical manifestation of Alisia's inherited magical heritage, (5) narrative framing through manual backstory rather than in-game cinematics (except victory). The game lacks explicit faction color coding or sigil systems; instead uses thematic visual language (lightning = Alisia's legacy, cocoon = evil presence, mixed creature types = corruption). Gainax's animation influence evident in character design (though redrawn for Western release) and environmental storytelling through artistic coherence.
